sutta:
monks there are these 4 perversions of perception 4 perversions of thought 4 perversions of view what for these perversions are called palasa in what for to hold and in the impermanent there is permanence is a perversion of perception thought and view to hold that in the ill there is not ill is a perversion of perception thought and view to hold that in the not cell there is self atta is a perversion of thought of perception thought and view to hold that in the repulsive there is the attractive subah is a perversion of perception thought and view these are the 4 proportions of perception thought and view monks there are these 4 non-perversions of perception thought and view what 4 to hold that in the impermanent there is impermanence that in the eel is ill that in the not self is not self that in the repulsive is the repulsive these are the non-perversions of perception thought and view these these this concerns the 3 characteristics of existence which are the impermanence [Music] and we have perversion of perception thought and view if we think that things in the world are different from these 3 characteristics of impermanence and then the last 1 is in the repulsive we think that it is attractive and this 1 mainly concerns like the human body to see in another human body the attraction especially of the opposite sex not to see things in the right perspective that's why sometimes the buddha asks us to meditate on the 32 parts of the body so that we can see a body in the right perspective not only the outside form of it but to view the inside also with all its intestines and heart and lungs and excrement and urine and blood etc so on these are the 4 proportions


===== SOURCE: 026_Anguttara Nikaya Book 4D 4550 - 4761 by Bhante Hye Dhammavuddho Mahathera.txt =====
now the next 2 ties
